titleInfo.title: The New-York directory
titleInfo.subTitle: containing, a valuable and well calculated almanack; tables of the different coins, suitable for any state, and digested in such order as to render an exchange between any of the United States plain and easy

originInfo.publisher: Printed by Shepard Kollock, corner of Wall and Water Streets

note: Modern quarter dark green leather and green cloth; binder's ticket on back pastedown: "Leather--full chrome. Neumann, Hoboken"; date stamped: Jul 11, 1938. NN

Incomplete; lacks title page and pages 5-6, supplied in facsimile. Inscribed on binder's blank at beginning: "The first New York directory. The title page and pp 5-6 are in facsimile by Bierstadt--from the N.Y. Historical Society copy. (1896)"

mods.tableOfContents: "Likewise, 1. The names of all the citizens, their occupations and places of abode, 2. The members in Congress, from what state, and where residing, 3. Grand departments of the United States for adjusting public accounts, and by whom conducted, 4. Members in Senate and Assembly, from what country, and where residing, 5. Judges, aldermen, and other civil officers, with their places of abode, 6. Public state-offices, and by whom kept, 7. Counsellors at law, and where residing, 8. Ministers of the gospel, where residing, and of what church, 9. Physicians, surgeons, and their places of abode, 10. President, directors, days and hours of business at the Bank, 11. Professors, &c. of the university of Columbia college, 12. Rates of porterage, as by law established, 13. Arrivals and departures of the mails at the Post-Office."
note: by David Franks.
Signatures: A-G⁶(-G6).
Also available online (New-York Historical Society copy) via Early American Imprints (Digital Evans) and Gateway to North America.

Indexed in: English Short Title Catalogue, W24848 ; Evans, C. American bibliography, 19655 ; Sabin, J. Dictionary of books relating to America from its discovery to the present time, 54456; Spear, D.N. Bibliography of American directories through 1860, page 231

titleInfo.title: New-York directory, and register, for the year 1789

originInfo.publisher: Printed for Hodge, Allen, and Campbell, and sold at their respective stores

note: "Illustrated with a new and accurate plan of the city of New-York, and part of Long-Island, including the suburbs, with all the streets, lanes, public buildings, wharves, &c., exactly laid down, from the latest survey"--Title page, 1789.

Issues for 1789-1790 lack statement of responsibility on title page. Statement by "the editors of this directory and register" on title page verso of issue for 1789 is signed: Robert Hodge, Thomas Allen, Samuel Campbell.

Extensive listing of contents on title page. In issue for 1789: "Containing, an alphabetical list of the names, occupations, and places of abode of the citizens, a register of the Congress of the United States, foreign ministers, governors of different states, officers of the State of New-York, officers in Chancery, Assembly, Senate, officers of city and county, Chamber of Commerce, Marine Society, Assurance Company, ministers of the Gospel, Medical Society, the other societies in the city, roll of attornies of Supreme Court, Columbia College, Library Society, militia officers, Masonic lodges, post days, stages, and coach rates, impost law. extracts from sundry laws for the regulation of trade, &c." Varies in subsequent issues.

Map on folded sheet at beginning of issues for 1789-1795; has caption title: Plan of the city of New York. In issue for 1789, signed: I.M. Comb Junr. [that is, John McComb, Jr.] delt., C. Tiebaut sculpt. 1789. In later issues, signed Tiebaut sculpt. without date. Two slightly varying engravings were used, respectively 21 x 43 cm and 21 x 37 cm.
Available online via Early American Imprints (Digital Evans) and Gateway to North America.

Map on folded sheet at beginning of issues for 1789-1795; has caption title: Plan of the city of New York. In issue for 1789, signed: I.M. Comb Junr. [that is, John McComb, Jr.] delt., C. Tiebaut sculpt. 1789. In later issues, signed Tiebaut sculpt. without date. Two slightly varying engravings were used, respectively 21 x 43 cm and 21 x 37 cm.
Publisher/bookseller information for Hodge, Allen, and Campbell varies. 1790: "... and sold at their several book-stores."

titleInfo.title: New-York directory, and register, for the year 1791

originInfo.publisher: Printed for the editor, by T. and J. Swords, no. 27, William-street

note: "Illustrated with a new and accurate plan of the city of New-York, and part of Long-Island, including the suburbs, with all the streets, lanes, public buildings, wharves, &c., exactly laid down, from the latest survey"--Title page, 1789. Varies in subsequent issues; 1790: "Illustrated with an accurate and elegant plan ... "; 1791-1795: "Illustrated with a new and accurate plan of the city, and part of Long-Island,  exactly laid down, agreeably to the latest survey"; 1796: "Illustrated with a plan of the city and part of Long-Island, laid down agreeably to the latest survey." Despite statement on title page, issue for 1796 was issued without the map.
Issues for 1791-1795 have statement of responsibility on title page: By William Duncan.
Issue for 1791: Directory printed by T. and J. Swords; registry (separately paged) printed by T. Greenleaf. See note on page v.

Extensive listing of contents on title page. In issue for 1789: "Containing, an alphabetical list of the names, occupations, and places of abode of the citizens, a register of the Congress of the United States, foreign ministers, governors of different states, officers of the State of New-York, officers in Chancery, Assembly, Senate, officers of city and county, Chamber of Commerce, Marine Society, Assurance Company, ministers of the Gospel, Medical Society, the other societies in the city, roll of attornies of Supreme Court, Columbia College, Library Society, militia officers, Masonic lodges, post days, stages, and coach rates, impost law. extracts from sundry laws for the regulation of trade, &c." Varies in subsequent issues.

Map on folded sheet at beginning of issues for 1789-1795; has caption title: Plan of the city of New York. In issue for 1789, signed: I.M. Comb Junr. [that is, John McComb, Jr.] delt., C. Tiebaut sculpt. 1789. In later issues, signed Tiebaut sculpt. without date. Two slightly varying engravings were used, respectively 21 x 43 cm and 21 x 37 cm.
Available online via Early American Imprints (Digital Evans) and Gateway to North America.

titleInfo.title: New-York directory, and register, for the year 1796

originInfo.publisher: Printed by John Buel, corner of Water-street and Fly-Market, and John Bull, 115 Cherry-street

note: "Illustrated with a plan of the city and part of Long-Island, laid down agreeably to the latest survey." Despite statement on title page, issue for 1796 was issued without the map.
"To which is added, the names of the inhabitants of Brooklyn, together with alphabetical account of the streets, lanes, wharves and slips of this city"--Title page, 1796.
Issue for 1796 has statement of responsibility on title page: By John Low.

Extensive listing of contents on title page. In issue for 1789: "Containing, an alphabetical list of the names, occupations, and places of abode of the citizens, a register of the Congress of the United States, foreign ministers, governors of different states, officers of the State of New-York, officers in Chancery, Assembly, Senate, officers of city and county, Chamber of Commerce, Marine Society, Assurance Company, ministers of the Gospel, Medical Society, the other societies in the city, roll of attornies of Supreme Court, Columbia College, Library Society, militia officers, Masonic lodges, post days, stages, and coach rates, impost law. extracts from sundry laws for the regulation of trade, &c." Varies in subsequent issues.
Issue for 1796 contains 1 folded leaf of plates, a double-page engraving representing "Flags of the different maritime nations." See preface on leaf following title page.
Available online via Early American Imprints (Digital Evans) and Gateway to North America.

titleInfo.title: Longworth's American almanack, New-York register, and city directory
Longworth's American almanack, New-York register, and city directory, for the twenty-second year of American independence
titleInfo.subTitle: containing most things useful in a work of the kind

originInfo.publisher: Printed for the editor, by T. & J. Swords, no. 99 Pearl-street

note: Other title information : "Containing most things useful in a work of the kind."
In some issues, almanac has special title page: 22nd-29th (1797-1804): Astronomical calculations for the ... year of American independence ... by Abraham Shoemaker.
Editors: 22nd-30th (1797-1805), David Longworth; 31st-67th (1806-1842), Thomas Longworth.
Compilers of almanac: 22nd-29th (1797-1804), Abraham Shoemaker; 33rd-42nd (1808-1817), Andrew Beers.

Some issues include engraved plates and/or other illustrative matter, some on folded leaves, for example, 22nd (1797): "A view of the new theatre in New York," signed: Tisdale delin. et sc.; 40th (1815) and others: "P.M. Stollenwerck's Mechanical Panorama," engraved by  T. Simonne, after J.B. Bourgoin.
Most issues include advertising.
Also issued on microfiche from Primary Source Microfilm, Woodbridge CT.
Also available online via HathiTrust and Google Books (selected issues only); Early American Imprints (22nd-44th, 1797-1819); and Gateway to North America (22nd-67th, 1797-1842).

titleInfo.title: Longworth's American almanack, New-York register, and city directory
Longworth's American almanack, New-York register, and city directory, for the twenty-fourth year of American independence

originInfo.publisher: Printed by John C. Totten and Co., no. 66, Nassau-Street

note: In some issues, almanac has special title page: 22nd-29th (1797-1804): Astronomical calculations for the ... year of American independence ... by Abraham Shoemaker; 31st-36th (1806-1811), Longworth's New-York almanack [later "almanac"]; for the ... year of American independence, commencing the fourth of July ...; 37th-42nd (1812-1817), Longworth's New-York almanac, for the year of our Lord ... [37th-40th: year following year of publication; 41st-42nd: inclusive years, as "1816-17"].

Editors: 22nd-30th (1797-1805), David Longworth; 31st-67th (1806-1842), Thomas Longworth.
Compilers of almanac: 22nd-29th (1797-1804), Abraham Shoemaker; 33rd-42nd (1808-1817), Andrew Beers.

Some issues include engraved plates and/or other illustrative matter, some on folded leaves, for example, 22nd (1797): "A view of the new theatre in New York," signed: Tisdale delin. et sc.; 40th (1815) and others: "P.M. Stollenwerck's Mechanical Panorama," engraved by  T. Simonne, after J.B. Bourgoin.
Most issues include advertising.

titleInfo.title: Longworth's American almanac, New-York register, and city directory
Longworth's American almanac, New-York register, and city directory
titleInfo.subTitle: for the twenty-fifth year of American independence

originInfo.publisher: Printed and published by D. Longworth

note: In some issues, almanac has special title page: 22nd-29th (1797-1804): Astronomical calculations for the ... year of American independence ... by Abraham Shoemaker; 31st-36th (1806-1811), Longworth's New-York almanack [later "almanac"]; for the ... year of American independence, commencing the fourth of July ...; 37th-42nd (1812-1817), Longworth's New-York almanac, for the year of our Lord ... [37th-40th: year following year of publication; 41st-42nd: inclusive years, as "1816-17"].

Some issues have special title page preceding register section, with title beginning: List of duties, payable by law on all goods, wares, and merchandise, imported into the United States of America, in American or foreign vessels ...
Editors: 22nd-30th (1797-1805), David Longworth; 31st-67th (1806-1842), Thomas Longworth.
Compilers of almanac: 22nd-29th (1797-1804), Abraham Shoemaker; 33rd-42nd (1808-1817), Andrew Beers.
Most issues include advertising.

Some issues have also chronological designation in cover title, for example "Longworth's American almanac, New-York register, and city directory for 1823-4,  being for the 48th year of American indepedence."
Also issued on microfiche from Primary Source Microfilm, Woodbridge CT.
Also available online via HathiTrust and Google Books (selected issues only); Early American Imprints (22nd-44th, 1797-1819); and Gateway to North America (22nd-67th, 1797-1842).

note: In some issues, almanac has special title page: 22nd-29th (1797-1804): Astronomical calculations for the ... year of American independence ... by Abraham Shoemaker; 31st-36th (1806-1811), Longworth's New-York almanack [later "almanac"]; for the ... year of American independence, commencing the fourth of July ...; 37th-42nd (1812-1817), Longworth's New-York almanac, for the year of our Lord ... [37th-40th: year following year of publication; 41st-42nd: inclusive years, as "1816-17"].

Some issues have special title page preceding register section, with title beginning: List of duties, payable by law on all goods, wares, and merchandise, imported into the United States of America, in American or foreign vessels ...
Editors: 22nd-30th (1797-1805), David Longworth; 31st-67th (1806-1842), Thomas Longworth.
Compilers of almanac: 22nd-29th (1797-1804), Abraham Shoemaker; 33rd-42nd (1808-1817), Andrew Beers.
Most issues include advertising.

Some issues have also chronological designation in cover title, for example "Longworth's American almanac, New-York register, and city directory for 1823-4,  being for the 48th year of American indepedence."
Also issued on microfiche from Primary Source Microfilm, Woodbridge CT.
Also available online via HathiTrust and Google Books (selected issues only); Early American Imprints (22nd-44th, 1797-1819); and Gateway to North America (22nd-67th, 1797-1842).

titleInfo.title: New York City (Manhattan and Bronx) directory
Polk's (Trow's) New York City directory (Boroughs of Manhattan and Bronx)

physicalLocation: Irma and Paul Milstein Division of United States History, Local History and Genealogy
Irma and Paul Milstein Division of United States History, Local History and Genealogy
Irma and Paul Milstein Division of United States History, Local History and Genealogy

note: Microfilm. 1929-1973/74. New York : New York Public Library, 1957-1976. 26 microfilm reels ; 35 mm. (MN *ZZAN-1588). Earlier directories, beginning with 1878, are on reels for Manhattan. Those reels also include other parts of the tri-state area.  See: New York City telephone directories.
Corrected to October 19, 1939.

physicalLocation: Irma and Paul Milstein Division of United States History, Local History and Genealogy
Irma and Paul Milstein Division of United States History, Local History and Genealogy

note:
Microfilm. 1929-1976/77. New York : New York Public Library, 1957-1976. 46 microfilm reels ; 35 mm. (MN *ZZAN-1588). Prior to 1944 also includes the Brooklyn classified directory. For 1944 and later issues of the classified directory, see: New York City telephone directories. Brooklyn. Classified.
For 1976 and later years consult issues on Phonefiche.
Corrected to January 4, 1940.

Ann Arbor, Mich. : University Microfilms International, 1976. 1 microfilm reel ; negative ; 35 mm; For 1976 and later years consult issues on Phonefiche.

note: Microfilm. 1927/1928-1973. New York : New York Public Library, 1957-1973. 15 microfilm reels ; 35 mm. (MN *ZZAN-1588), Filmed together with the Staten Island classified; Microfilm. 1974-1976. Ann Arbor, Mich. : University Microfilms International, 1976. 3 microfilm reels ; 35 mm; For 1976 and later years consult issues on Phonefiche.
Corrected up to January 9, 1940.

physicalLocation: Irma and Paul Milstein Division of United States History, Local History and Genealogy
Irma and Paul Milstein Division of United States History, Local History and Genealogy

note: Microfilm. winter 1928-1987/88. New York : New York Public Library, 1957-1988. 57 microfilm reels ; 35 mm. (MN *ZZAN-1588). Winter 1928 is a combined Manhattan/ Bronx directory. Earlier years, beginning with 1878, are on reels that include all of New York City and other parts of the tri-state area. See: New York City telephone directories.
Corrected to November 9, 1939.
